description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who is preparing a tiny “ground-truth” dataset that confirms
    basic DNS / hostname resolution works inside the current Linux environment.
    Follow the steps below strictly.

    1. Create the directory “/home/user/ml_data/dns_test”.
    2. Inside that directory create a CSV file named “localhost_ip.csv”.
       • The file MUST contain exactly two lines (a header line plus one data line).  
       • The header line must be:
         hostname,ip_address
       • The second line must be:
         localhost,127.0.0.1
       • There must be **no spaces** before or after the commas.  
       • Terminate the file with a single UNIX newline (line-feed) after the second line.
    3. Display the file to the console so that automated tests can see its contents.

    The automated grader will check ONLY:
       • that the directory “/home/user/ml_data/dns_test” exists,
       • that “/home/user/ml_data/dns_test/localhost_ip.csv” exists,
       • that the file’s exact byte content matches the two-line specification above,
       • and that the file ends with a single trailing newline.

    You do not need root privileges; everything should be done as the normal user.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/ml_data/dns_test
       (permissions may be the default 755; ownership user:user)

    2. File:
       /home/user/ml_data/dns_test/localhost_ip.csv
       Expected content (including the final line-feed):
       ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │hostname,ip_address\n                            │
       │localhost,127.0.0.1\n                            │
       └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
       • File size must therefore be 42 bytes (assuming LF as newline).
       • Recommended permissions 644; ownership user:user.

    No other files or directories need to be created or modified.
